world.title: Widespread 'No Kings' Rallies Across U.S. See Arrests Amid Protests Against Trump, Iran War, Immigration, Cost Of Living
world.description: Large protests against the Trump administration erupted across the United States on Saturday, marking the third round of the No Kings rallies, which have previously drawn millions of participants.  Organisers say they are protesting against US President Donald Trump’s policies, including the war in Iran, federal immigration enforcement, and the rising cost of living. "Trump wants to rule over us as a tyrant. But this is America, and power belongs to the people, not to wannabe kings or their billionaire cronies," organisers said.

world.title: ‘Nuclear fatwa is dead’: Is Iran now racing toward a bomb after the war?
world.description: Tehran’s claim its nuclear program is peaceful relied on Khamenei’s religious ban; his successor has not reaffirmed it, hardliners are rising, and calls grow to pursue a bomb: 'We have entered a new phase, we are a superpower'

world.title: Europe seeks to increase deportations, quietly adopting Trump administration tactics
world.description: The European Union is expanding its powers to track, raid and deport migrants to "return hubs″ in third countries in Africa and elsewhere, quietly adopting tactics of the Trump administration.

science.title: Laser fluence dependent antibacterial activity of cadmium sulfide quantum dots prepared by one step laser ablation in liquid
science.description: Cadmium sulfide (CdS) quantum dots (QDs) were synthesized using pulsed laser ablation in liquid (PLAL) at different laser fluences. The effect of laser fluence on the optical, structural, morphological, and electrical properties of CdS QDs was investigated by using X-ray diffraction (XRD), field emission scanning electron microscopy (FESEM), energy-dispersive X-ray (EDX), zeta potential, photoluminescence (PL), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), UV–Visible absorption spectroscopy, and Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR). XRD analysis show that the synthesized CdS QDs have a hexagonal wurzite structure and the crystallite size was in the range of 6–11 nm. They also exhibit negative surface charge about − 2.7, − 18.2, and − 19.0 mV, all that for CdS QDs, which were prepared at 1.27, 3.82, and 7.64 J/cm², laser fluences, respectively. The antibacterial activity of the CdS QDs was evaluated against both Gram-negative bacteria (Escherichia coli, Pseudomonas aeruginosa) and Gram-positive bacteria (Staphylococcus aureus, Streptococcus agalactiae). The results showed varying inhibition zones depending on the concentration, surface deformation, and particle size of the CdS QDs, in which the smallest size QDs, which were prepared by using 7.64 J/cm² laser fluence, show higher inhibition zones than other particles. In addition, the minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) was determined for each bacterial strain.
